message:
chore(beads): close process_triage-ed3.4 and bd-3jpe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3dbm","title":"Bench: synthetic quick-scan throughput (10k lines)","description":"## Goal
Add a deterministic macro benchmark for the quick-scan hot path without relying on real system processes.

## Scope
- Build a synthetic ps-like output with 10k lines (Linux + macOS variants if feasible) and benchmark parsing into ProcessRecords.
- Or: build 10k ProcessRecords directly and benchmark the scan->filter->candidate selection steps that do not require /proc.
- Add generous CI budget checks to perf smoke.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Criterion bench runs in CI deterministically and completes fast (<1s in smoke mode).
- Budget check exists to catch order-of-magnitude regressions.
- Bench inputs are deterministic and redaction-safe (no real paths).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireSpring","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:06:48.457008349Z","created_by":"SapphireSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:23:49.070433395Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:23:49.070369777Z","close_reason":"Implemented synthetic quick-scan parsing bench + perf smoke budget (see d87da04)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dbm","depends_on_id":"process_triage-5g8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:06:48.514179890Z","created_by":"SapphireSpring"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gkt","title":"No-mock supervision detection tests (systemd/launchd/container/agent)","description":"## Goal
Validate supervision detection + routing with real data (no mocks); provide fixtures consumed by E2E supervision gating tests.

## Scope
- Unit tests for systemd/launchd parsing, cgroup/container supervision markers, agent supervision detection.
- Integration tests on fixture snapshots for expected supervisor types.
- Produce supervision fixtures + expected routing metadata for E2E bead bd-12r5.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per case: event, timestamp, supervisor_type, pid, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Store fixtures under `test/fixtures/supervision/` (sanitized + manifest).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit coverage for detection/parsing helpers without mocks.
- [ ] Integration coverage on real host or fixtures (platform-conditional).
- [ ] Fixtures + logs are consumable by bd-12r5 E2E script.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per case.

## Test Plan
- Unit: parse outputs from systemctl/launchctl and cgroup markers.
- Integration: run detection on fixture snapshots and validate expected supervisor types.

 {"id":"bd-3hb3","title":"E2E: install/update/rollback scripts with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Add comprehensive E2E scripts for install.sh + update + rollback with verbose logging.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Containerized install tests (clean system, verify success).
- Tampered binary/invalid checksum failure path.
- Update + rollback flows with version verification.
- JSONL logs + checksums + timing metrics captured as artifacts.
- Cross-platform matrix where possible (linux/mac/windows runners).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, checksums, artifacts[].
- Artifact bundle: logs + checksums + resolved version info.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E install scripts pass across supported platforms/containers.
- [ ] Fail paths are validated and logged clearly.
- [ ] Artifacts include logs + checksums + versions.
- [ ] JSONL schema validation passes.

## Test Plan
- E2E: docker-based runs for install/update/rollback.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.212974556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:21.449151353Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:21.449114695Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.220380171Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:30.967761891Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.228792047Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-ica.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.234889641Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3jpe","title":"Add differential and goal-oriented options to agent plan","description":"## Task
Add differential and goal-oriented option parsing to `pt agent plan` for API surface harmonization.

**SCOPE LIMITATION**: This bead implements CLI flag parsing only. Full feature implementation is deferred to:
- Differential mode: process_triage-9k8.2 (EPIC: Phase 11 - Differential Analysis)
- Goal-oriented: process_triage-95m.* (EPIC: Phase 13 - Goal-Oriented Optimization)
- Predictions: process_triage-yxt.* (EPIC: Phase 12 - Trajectory Prediction)

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent plan [OPTIONS]
  --session <id>           Resume existing session
  --max-candidates <N>     Maximum candidates
  --min-posterior <f64>    Minimum posterior (alias: --threshold)
  --only <filter>          Filter by recommendation
  --deep                   Force deep scan
  --min-age <seconds>      Minimum process age
```

## New Options (Stub Implementation for v1)

### Differential Mode Flags
```
--since <session-id>       Compare against prior session
--since-time <ts|dur>      Compare against time (e.g., "2h", "2026-01-15T12:00:00Z")
```

### Goal-Oriented Flags
```
--goal "<target>"          Resource recovery goal (format: "free 4GB RAM")
```

### Predictive Mode Flags
```
--include-predictions      Add trajectory analysis to output
```

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1 (This Bead): Parse flags, return not-implemented warning
```rust
// Add to AgentPlanArgs
#[arg(long, help = "Compare against prior session (coming in v1.2)")]
since: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Compare against time (coming in v1.2)")]
since_time: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Resource recovery goal (coming in v1.2)")]
goal: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Add trajectory analysis (coming in v1.2)")]
include_predictions: bool,
```

### Behavior for v1 (Stub)
When any of these flags are used, the plan is generated normally BUT includes a warning:

```json
{
  "session_id": "pt-20260122-...",
  "candidates": [...],
  "stub_flags": {
    "warning": "feature_not_implemented",
    "message": "Some flags are parsed but not yet functional",
    "flags_used": ["--since"],
    "flags_ignored": ["--since"],
    "workaround": "Use pt agent diff for manual comparison",
    "planned_release": "v1.2"
  }
}
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Exit code: 0 (success with warning, not error)
- Plan is still generated (flags are ignored, not blocking)
- Warning appears in both JSON and summary formats
- Agents can discover the API surface via --help
- Scripts using these flags degrade gracefully

### Phase 2 (Future Beads): Full implementation
- Differential: Depends on session comparison infrastructure
- Goal-oriented: Depends on optimization engine
- Predictions: Depends on trajectory modeling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--since`, `--since-time`, `--goal`, `--include-predictions` flags parse correctly
- [ ] Using these flags returns normal plan WITH warning, not error
- [ ] Help text documents flags with "(coming in v1.2)" notes
- [ ] Exit code 0 even when using stub flags
- [ ] Plan output unchanged when flags not used
- [ ] Warning in both JSON and summary formats
- [ ] `stub_flags` section clearly lists which flags were used and ignored

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_since_flag() {
    // --since value is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_goal_flag() {
    // --goal "free 4GB RAM" is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_produces_warning() {
    // Using --since results in stub_flags warning in output
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_still_generates_plan() {
    // Plan candidates still present when stub flags used
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_no_warning_without_stub_flags() {
    // Normal plan without stub flags has no stub_flags section
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_plan_future_flags.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for plan command future flags (stub implementation)

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_plan_future_flags_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: plan future flags (stubs) ==="

# Test --since flag parsing
log "Testing --since flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since "fake-session" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

log "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

# Verify exit code is 0 (success with warning)
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Exit code 0 (success with warning)"
else
    log "⚠ Exit code $EXIT_CODE (may be acceptable if error is clear)"
fi

# Verify warning is present
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags.warning == \"feature_not_implemented\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --since returns not-implemented warning"
    USED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".stub_flags.flags_used | join(\", \")")
    log "  Flags used: $USED"
elif echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Warning present (different structure)"
else
    log "✗ --since should return warning"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | head -20 >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify plan is still generated
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".session_id or .candidates" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Plan still generated despite stub flag"
else
    log "⚠ Plan might not be generated (check output)"
fi

# Test --goal flag parsing  
log "Testing --goal flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --goal "free 4GB RAM" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --goal accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --goal exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --goal returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --goal should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-predictions flag
log "Testing --include-predictions flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --include-predictions --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --include-predictions exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --include-predictions should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --since-time flag
log "Testing --since-time flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since-time "2h" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --since-time accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --since-time exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

# Test normal plan has no warning
log "Testing normal plan (no stub flags)..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "⚠ Normal plan has stub_flags (should not)"
else
    log "✓ Normal plan has no stub_flags section"
fi

# Test help mentions stub flags
log "Testing help output..."
HELP=$(pt-core agent plan --help 2>&1)
if echo "$HELP" | grep -q "since"; then
    log "✓ Help mentions --since"
else
    log "✗ Help should mention --since"
    exit 1
fi

if echo "$HELP" | grep -qi "coming\|v1.2\|future\|planned"; then
    log "✓ Help indicates feature is upcoming"
else
    log "⚠ Help might not indicate feature is upcoming"
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:38:26.764582930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:15:28.652172776Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jpe","depends_on_id":"bd-v5ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:54Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-3jpe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented stub flags for --since, --since-time, --goal, --include-predictions.  {"id":"bd-3k9b","title":"E2E: bundle encryption + verification flows","description":"## Goal
Validate bundle encryption/decryption and checksum verification end-to-end with detailed logging and failure-path coverage.

## Scope
- `pt bundle create --encrypt` with passphrase arg and env var.
- `pt bundle inspect` + `extract` with verification on/off.
- Failure modes: wrong passphrase, corrupted bundle, missing manifest.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: encrypted bundle, inspect output, extract output, manifest, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Encrypted bundles decrypt with correct passphrase only.
- [ ] Verification detects corruption.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per case.

## Test Plan
- E2E script generates bundle from fixture session and exercises encrypt/decrypt/verify.

 {"id":"bd-3kk2","title":"No-mock inference + ledger regression suite","description":"## Goal
Build a no-mock, deterministic regression suite for inference/posterior math and the evidence ledger.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Capture **realistic evidence fixtures** from live runs (CPU/IO/TTY/orphan/parent/cgroup/net), store as versioned test data.
- End-to-end inference pipeline on fixtures (collection features -> posterior -> confidence -> ledger), **no mocks**.
- Golden outputs for:
  - posterior class scores + confidence labels
  - top evidence factors with contributions and ordering
  - any derived summaries used by agent/TUI outputs
- Property tests:
  - posterior normalization (sum≈1)
  - monotonicity for key signals (e.g., longer idle increases P(abandoned))
  - sensitivity to missing evidence (graceful degradation)
- Numeric stability regression tests (NaN/Inf handling, underflow protection).

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use recorded fixtures and **actual inference code paths**; no trait mocks or fake implementations.
- Deterministic seeds and fixed fixtures for repeatability.
- Golden output updates require explicit approval (guardrail against drifting expectations).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per test case: fixture_id, posterior summary, top factors, confidence labels, runtime.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, case_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Redact/normalize any sensitive command strings in logs.
- Store artifacts under `test/artifacts/inference/<case>/` for CI upload.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/inference_e2e.bats` that runs `pt agent plan` on a fixture session and validates output schema + ledger summary.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inference on fixtures produces stable outputs (golden tests).
- [ ] Posterior outputs normalized and within defined tolerance.
- [ ] Evidence ledger ordering and contributions validated.
- [ ] Failure modes (NaN/Inf/empty evidence) covered deterministically.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per test case with stable schema.
- [ ] E2E fixture plan run validates output schema + ledger summary.

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden inference cases + property checks.
- Integration: pipeline from fixture -> features -> posterior -> ledger.
- E2E: fixture plan run with JSONL logging.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.073333494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:29.232381500Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:29.232311591Z","close_reason":"Implemented inference fixtures + nomock regression suite + E2E test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:27.429597853Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.086052876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.093432894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:21.790324959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:45:24.660814698Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lux","title":"No-mock wrapper workflow tests (pt bash wrapper, real processes)","description":"## Goal
Add **no-mock** coverage for the bash `pt` wrapper using real processes and the real `pt-core` binary, with standardized JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Build or download a real `pt-core` binary for test runs (no stubs).
- Spawn short-lived real processes (sleep loops, IO-bound, CPU-bound) and run `pt scan` / `pt agent plan` against them.
- Validate wrapper discovery logic (`PT_CORE_PATH`, PATH search, local `target/release`).
- Exercise wrapper update path in dry-run mode (no network) and ensure safe error messaging when tools missing.
- Verify wrapper respects `--version`, `--help`, and forwards args without mutation.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Do **not** stub `ps`, `kill`, or `pt-core` in PATH.
- Use real processes + real binaries; rely on harness to clean up safely.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per case: event, timestamp, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact bundle: `stdout`, `stderr`, `plan.json`, and harness metadata for each case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wrapper works end-to-end against real `pt-core` in CI.
- [ ] Real processes are detected and included in scan/plan output deterministically.
- [ ] Discovery and forwarding logic validated across PT_CORE_PATH/PATH scenarios.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- Integration: spawn real processes; run wrapper commands via harness.
- E2E: `pt scan`, `pt agent plan`, `pt --version`, update error paths.
- Logging: validate JSONL schema + artifact manifest.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:23.918040317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:29:21.826141118Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:29:21.826070908Z","close_reason":"Aligned wrapper workflow tests to pt-core; added nomock/e2e rust suites + fixtures; BATS + cargo tests green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:25.839658874Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:24.653063622Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:24.851321944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:25.565008578Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:25.123409208Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"process_triage-ed3.1","title":"Implement user signature customization (signatures.json + add/remove/list)","description":"## Context
Phase 11 (Pattern/Signature Library).

## Problem
Users need to teach pt about their own tools and workflows. A curated library is not enough; we need first-class user customization.

## Requirements
- Config file: `~/.config/process_triage/signatures.json`
- Commands:
  - `pt signature list`
  - `pt signature add` (guided, validates pattern)
  - `pt signature remove`
- Merge behavior:
  - built-in signatures + user signatures; user overrides allowed
- Validation:
  - schema validation
  - test match preview (`--dry-run` match against current processes)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User signatures load and merge deterministically.
- [ ] CLI commands work and are documented.
- [ ] Invalid signatures fail with actionable errors.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: merge/override behavior.
- E2E: create user signatures and confirm matches.

 {"id":"process_triage-ed3.2","title":"Implement signature-informed inference fast path (bypass/priors)","description":"## Context
Phase 11.

## Problem
Full inference can be expensive. When a process matches a high-confidence signature (e.g., known dev server pattern), we should be able to:
- skip deep inference
- or use signature-provided priors to accelerate and improve calibration

## Requirements
- Signature match returns a confidence score.
- If confidence ≥ threshold:
  - fast-path classification/prior override
  - still produce an evidence ledger (“matched signature X”)
- Track fast-path usage in telemetry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] High-confidence matches trigger fast-path behavior.
- [ ] Output remains explainable and auditable.
- [ ] Fast-path can be disabled via config/policy.

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: match confidence thresholds.
- Golden tests: known signature fixtures.

 {"id":"process_triage-ed3.3","title":"Track and calibrate signature match confidence","description":"## Context
Phase 11.

## Problem
Signature confidence scores must be calibrated; otherwise we risk false certainty and unsafe recommendations.

## Approach
- Persist signature match outcomes:
  - match id, confidence score, eventual human decision/outcome
- Compute calibration metrics in shadow mode:
  - reliability curves for match confidence
  - confusion matrix by signature category
- Provide tooling to adjust thresholds and identify bad signatures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Telemetry contains sufficient fields to analyze signature performance.
- [ ] Produces a calibration report artifact (DuckDB query or HTML section).

## Test Plan
- Unit tests: aggregation and metric computation on fixtures.
","notes":"Implemented: (1) calibrate submodules (metrics.rs, curve.rs, bias.rs, report.rs, queries.rs) with Brier/ECE/AUC-ROC metrics, calibration curves, bias detection, and DuckDB queries; (2) signature_matches telemetry table for calibration data; (3) signature tracking fields in proc_inference schema","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus4","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:56:06.714969685Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:42:52.642859921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:42:52.642699017Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-ed3.3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-79x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"process_triage-erz","title":"Implement pt agent snapshot command","description":"## Overview
Implement the `pt agent snapshot` command that captures system state and creates a session for subsequent operations.

## From Plan Section 3.5

### Command Purpose
Returns a session_id and system state snapshot for subsequent agent operations. This is typically the first command in an agent workflow.

### Output Contract
```json
{
  'session_id': 'abc123',
  'host_id': 'devbox1',
  'timestamp': '2025-01-15T10:30:00Z',
  'system_state': {
    'load': [5.17, 4.89, 10.23],
    'cores': 64,
    'memory': {'total_gb': 499, 'used_gb': 281, 'available_gb': 218},
    'process_count': 412,
    'psi': {'cpu': 0.02, 'memory': 0.05, 'io': 0.01}
  },
  'capabilities': {
    'tools': {'perf': true, 'bpftrace': false, 'strace': true, ...},
    'permissions': {'can_sudo': false, 'can_ptrace': true}
  }
}
```

### Session Creation
- Generate unique session_id
- Create artifact directory: `~/.local/share/process_triage/sessions/<session_id>/`
- Persist initial snapshot

### Workflow Integration
```
Agent invocation 1: pt agent snapshot
  → Returns session_id, system state, capabilities
  → Agent decides: 'need to clean up, let me plan'

Agent invocation 2: pt agent plan --session <id>
  → Reuses session context from snapshot
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Command generates unique session_id
- [ ] System state captured accurately
- [ ] Capabilities detected and reported
- [ ] Session artifact directory created
- [ ] JSON output is well-formed
- [ ] --format json/md supported

## Dependencies
- Phase 1 (session model, CLI surface)

## Technical Notes
- System state collection should be fast (< 100ms)
- Capabilities detection uses cached results where available
- Session IDs should be short and URL-safe","notes":"Reviewed agent snapshot implementation (main.rs agent snapshot + session persistence tests in crates/pt-core/tests/e2e_snapshot.rs / e2e_cli_scenarios.rs). Dependencies (t6lf, qje, 3ir) closed; ready to close.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"EmeraldEagle","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:53:27.253564573Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T05:11:20.741474617Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T05:11:20.741337522Z","close_reason":"Completed: agent snapshot command implemented + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-3ir","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-bwn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-qje","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-erz","depends_on_id":"process_triage-t6lf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-ew5y","title":"Implement pt agent apply command","description":"**Purpose**: Implement the `pt agent apply` command that executes actions from a plan.

**Plan Document Reference**: Section 3.5.1 - Agent CLI Contract

**CLI Surface**:
```
pt agent apply --session <id> [OPTIONS]
```

**Required Options**:
- `--session <id>` - Session containing the plan to execute
- `--recommended` - Apply all recommended actions from the plan
- `--pids 123,456` - Apply to specific PIDs (must exist in session plan)
- `--targets 123:<start_id>,456:<start_id>` - Explicit identity tuples (preferred)
- `--yes` - Required for execution (explicit confirmation)
- `--resume` - Resume an interrupted session from where it left off

**Confidence-Bounded Automation** (`--robot` controls):
- `--min-posterior <threshold>` - Only act if posterior > threshold (e.g., 0.99)
- `--max-blast-radius <amount>` - Limit total impact per run (e.g., "2GB", "5 processes")
- `--max-kills <N>` - Limit number of kill actions per run
- `--require-known-signature` - Only act on pattern library matches
- `--only-categories <list>` - Only act on specified categories
- `--exclude-categories <list>` - Never act on specified categories
- `--abort-on-unknown` - Stop if any unexpected condition encountered

**Safety Requirements**:
- MUST revalidate process identity immediately before applying any action
- If `(pid,start_id,uid)` mismatches, MUST block and require fresh plan
- MUST respect `--shadow` and `--dry-run` modes (no actual actions)
- MUST acquire per-user "pt lock" before executing
- MUST support supervisor-aware actions when supervisor detected

**Resumability**:
- Sessions track: plan, applied actions, pending actions, outcomes
- `--resume` continues from where interrupted
- Idempotent: re-running a completed action is a no-op

**Exit Codes**:
- 2: Actions executed successfully
- 3: Partial failure executing actions
- 4: Blocked by safety gates / policy
- 6: Session interrupted / resumable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the described scope with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).
- [ ] Includes unit/integration/E2E coverage for the highest-risk paths.

 {"id":"process_triage-f5o","title":"Implement progress event emission system","description":"## Task
Implement the progress event system that reports collection stages to TUI and agent CLI.

## Background
Both TUI and agent CLI need to show progress:
- scan_started, scan_progress, scan_complete
- deep_scan_started, deep_scan_progress, deep_scan_complete
- infer_started, infer_complete
- decide_started, decide_complete
- plan_ready

Events enable:
- TUI progress visualization
- pt agent tail --format jsonl streaming
- Timeout detection (no events = hung)

## Event Structure
{
  "event": "scan_progress",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T14:30:22Z",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "phase": "quick_scan",
  "progress": {"current": 150, "total": 312},
  "details": {"pids_scanned": 150}
}

## Implementation Notes
- Use channels for event dispatch
- Support multiple subscribers (TUI, file, network)
- Events should be cheap (not slow down collection)
- Include timing information for performance analysis

## Event Types
- session_started, session_ended
- quick_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- deep_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- inference_{started,progress,complete}
- decision_{started,complete}
- action_{started,complete,failed}
- plan_ready

## Deliverables
- Rust module: events/mod.rs
- Event type definitions
- Event emitter trait and implementations
- JSONL formatter for CLI
- Unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.
